methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Red 6, a Series B+ defense and security company that has raised over $100M to date, operates within a sector containing 19 identified peers. However, valuation by peer median is not applicable here: the analysis identified zero same-stage comparables, zero adjacent-stage comparables, and zero similar-valuation-tier peers meeting minimum threshold criteria of three or more datapoints. A sector-wide median would introduce excessive noise and obscure meaningful comparison.

The available comp universe comprises 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private entities. The top private peers include Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems. These companies span varying stages and valuations within defense technology, but the fragmentation—both in maturity and business model—prevents reliable peer-median triangulation.

Given the absence of sufficiently tight comparables, alternative valuation methods (DCF, precedent transaction analysis, or revenue multiples applied to disclosed metrics) would be required to establish a defensible range. The core constraint is stage and scale specificity: Red 6 occupies a narrow slice of the defense sector where truly analogous companies at Series B+ with comparable capital deployment are rare. Any valuation synthesis must acknowledge this comp scarcity and rely on alternative methodologies or explicit scenario modeling rather than peer-based inference.
